# IL-42

## Summary

IL-42 is a candidate immune-modulating cytokine that appears capable of suppressing activated CD8 effector function. The strongest current evidence links IL-42 to reduced granzyme B output rather than complete shutdown of early activation markers.

## Key Findings

- [supports] IL-42 is described as an immune-modulating cytokine in activated T-cell systems. [[zhang-2024-il42-overview]]
- [extends] Recombinant IL-42 reduced granzyme B-positive activated primary CD8 cells by about 38 percent in a 24-hour assay. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]
- [extends] IL-42 lowered IFN-gamma while leaving CD69 nearly unchanged, which suggests selective suppression of effector output. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]
- [extends] Neutralizing IL-42 restored granzyme B toward baseline in the same assay system. [[lee-2026-il42-cd8-suppression]]

## Contradictions and Caveats

- The receptor for IL-42 is still unknown.
- Current evidence is in vitro and limited to one short time window.

## Open Questions

- Which receptor complex senses IL-42?
- Is IL-42 broadly suppressive across T-cell subsets or mainly CD8-focused?
